page: Amazon Web Services c7a.24xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features 96 dedicated physical cores and 96 vCPUs using the x86_64 architecture, driven by an AMD EPYC 9R14 processor running at 3.7 GHz. The processor includes 32 KB L1d cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 32 MB L3 cache per core, alongside advanced instruction support such as AVX-512 (including VNNI and BF16) and cryptographic acceleration (AES, VAES, SHA_NI). The instance is equipped with 192.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory operating at 5 GHz, providing a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and features a baseline network bandwidth of 38 Gbps.

Benchmark data highlights the instance's strong multi-core execution capabilities. In multi-core stress-ng and Geekbench tests, the c7a.24xlarge ranks in the top 10% of tested systems. Memory performance is similarly elevated, with read-only cached small block and uncached large block memory bandwidth tests both placing in the top 10%. The system also demonstrates top-tier performance in specialized computational tasks, including software compilation with Clang, ray tracing, image processing, and Passmark CPU encryption and physics tests. For database and key-value operations, the instance achieves top-tier results in Redis SET operations and strong performance in Passmark database operations.

The instance exhibits notable capabilities in large language model (LLM) inference. Prompt processing speeds for small (135M), medium (7B), and large (70B) models rank in the top 10% to top 25%, while text generation speeds for medium and large models consistently reach top-tier levels. Given the 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and the absence of local storage, this instance is highly optimized for compute-bound workloads. It is well-suited for high-performance computing (HPC), batch processing, software compilation, scientific modeling, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
description: Amazon Web Services c7a.24xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 96 dedicated physical cores and 96 vCPUs on the x86_64 architecture, powered by an AMD EPYC 9R14 processor running at 3.7 GHz. It is equipped with 192.0 GB of DDR5 memory at 5 GHz, yielding a 2.0 GB per core ratio, and operates on the Nitro hypervisor with a baseline network bandwidth of 38 Gbps. The instance does not include local storage or GPUs. Benchmark results place the c7a.24xlarge in the top 10% for multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth, and software compilation. It also demonstrates strong capabilities in LLM prompt processing and text generation. This hardware configuration is highly suited for compute-intensive workloads, including high-performance computing, software compilation, batch processing, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c7a.24xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 96 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 192 GB of DDR5 memory, and 38 Gbps network bandwidth, designed for high-performance multi-core workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c7a.24xlarge is a compute-optimized instance with 96 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 192 GB DDR5 RAM, and top-tier multi-core performance.
tagline: A high-core-count, compute-optimized platform featuring DDR5 memory and advanced vector instructions for demanding multi-threaded processing workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a 96-core AMD EPYC 9R14 processor running at 3.7 GHz
Equipped with 192.0 GB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory
Delivers top-tier multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth performance
Features 38 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the Nitro hypervisor
Optimized for CPU-based LLM inference, software compilation, and HPC workloads
